task_completion.description: Open the 'Mitosis' sample dataset in ImageJ/Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji interface showing the 'Mitosis' image window. The image status bar should indicate it is a hyperstack (e.g., 171x196 pixels, 2 channels, 5 slices, 51 frames).
A new image window titled 'MAX_Mitosis' or similar, indicating a Z-projection was created. The Z-slice slider (usually labeled 'z') should no longer be present or should indicate 1 slice.
The active image window shows only the red channel (often titled 'C1-MAX_Mitosis' if channels were split). The green channel should be closed or inactive.

task_completion.description: Create a sub-stack containing only the first 20 timepoints
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image window's time slider (labeled 't' or 'frame') shows exactly 20 frames total (e.g., '1/20' or '20/20' in the status bar) instead of the original 51.

task_completion.description: Add a timestamp starting at 00:00 with a 15.00-second interval in the top-left corner
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible timestamp in the top-left corner of the image frames (e.g., '00:00', '00:15', '00:30') rendered in a font size of approximately 20-25.

task_completion.description: Save the final TIFF stack to the specified processed directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box or file explorer/terminal confirming the file is saved exactly as 'mitosis_movie_formatted.tif' in the '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/processed/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the ImageJ/Fiji GUI to perform the image processing tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through ImageJ menus (e.g., Image > Stacks > Z Project, Image > Color > Split Channels, Image > Stacks > Tools > Make Substack) rather than executing a single terminal command.

integrity.description: Agent generated intermediate image windows during processing
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate windows like 'MAX_Mitosis' or 'C1-MAX_Mitosis' proving the agent actually performed the projection and channel splitting steps manually.

integrity.description: Agent did not use external command-line tools (like ImageMagick or FFmpeg) to bypass ImageJ
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run image processing commands; all visual modifications (cropping frames, adding timestamps) happen within the ImageJ environment.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original Mitosis sample is a 171x196 pixel 5D hyperstack with 51 frames, 5 Z-slices, and 2 channels. The final expected output must be saved to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/processed/mitosis_movie_formatted.tif'. It should have exactly 20 frames, 1 channel (Red), and a timestamp in the top-left corner incrementing by 15 seconds per frame.
